content_id:
Tokoh-tokoh yang mendapat perlakuan paling luas dalam Sifat as-Safwah adalah mereka yang mewujudkan tradisi-tradisi Sufi dan asketik Sunni dalam bentuknya yang paling terkemuka. Membaca catatan-catatan ini mengungkap tema-tema spiritual yang dianggap Ibn al-Jawzi, mengikuti Abu Nu'aym, paling khas dari keunggulan Islam.

Para sahabat mendapat perlakuan rinci pada bagian-bagian awal. Kualitas-kualitas spiritual mereka — kedalaman iman para sahabat Nabi, pengorbanan mereka, dzikir mereka yang terus-menerus kepada Allah — didokumentasikan melalui catatan-catatan tentang shalat mereka, doa-doa mereka, tangisan mereka karena takut kepada Allah, dan perlakuan mereka terhadap sesama. Para sahabat disajikan bukan hanya sebagai tokoh-tokoh historis semata, melainkan sebagai model-model hidup tentang bagaimana pesan Nabi mengubah jiwa-jiwa manusia.

Tokoh-tokoh seperti al-Hasan al-Basri, Ibrahim ibn Adham, Sufyan ats-Tsauri, dan Fudhail ibn Iyad mendapat perlakuan ekstensif. Mereka adalah tokoh-tokoh yang perpaduan antara ilmu keagamaan dan kedalaman spiritual menjadikan mereka teladan bagi generasi-generasi berikutnya. Al-Hasan al-Basri khususnya menempati tempat sentral dalam tradisi asketik yang didokumentasikan dalam Sifat as-Safwah: pernyataan-pernyataannya yang terkenal tentang sifat dunia yang fana, pentingnya muhasabah spiritual, dan kualitas-kualitas yang diperlukan untuk kesalehan yang sejati dilestarikan dalam puluhan catatan dan memberikan pengaruh yang membentuk spiritualitas Islam berikutnya.

Penyertaan perempuan di antara tokoh-tokoh terkemuka dalam Sifat as-Safwah sangatlah signifikan. Rabi'ah al-Adawiyyah dari Basrah, zahidah terkenal yang mengartikulasikan teologi cinta murni kepada Allah, mendapat perlakuan yang luas. Catatan-catatannya — doanya yang terkenal bahwa ia mencintai Allah bukan karena takut neraka atau berharap surga melainkan murni karena-Nya sendiri — menjadi pusat literatur spiritual Islam. Pelestarian Ibn al-Jawzi atas catatan-catatan ini dalam format biografi membuatnya dapat diakses lintas generasi.

Tema-tema spiritual yang berulang sepanjang Sifat as-Safwah meliputi: pemupukan ihsan (keunggulan spiritual, atau beribadah kepada Allah seolah-olah engkau melihat-Nya); praktik muhasabah (hisab diri, atau pemeriksaan terus-menerus atas kondisi dan niat batin seseorang); pentingnya zuhud (asketisme atau menahan diri dari kelekatan duniawi); kebajikan sabar (kesabaran dalam menghadapi cobaan); dan sentralitas dzikir (dzikir kepada Allah) sebagai praktik yang menghidupkan kehidupan spiritual.
content_en:
The figures who receive the most extensive treatment in Sifat as-Safwah are those who embodied the Sunni Sufi and ascetic traditions at their most distinguished. Reading through these accounts reveals the spiritual themes that Ibn al-Jawzi, following Abu Nu'aym, considered most characteristic of Islamic excellence.

The companions receive detailed treatment in the early sections. Their spiritual qualities — the profundity of the Prophet's companions' faith, their sacrifice, their continuous remembrance of God — are documented through accounts of their prayers, their supplications, their weeping from fear of God, and their treatment of others. The companions are presented not as historical figures only but as living models of how the prophetic message transformed human souls.

Figures like al-Hasan al-Basri, Ibrahim ibn Adham, Sufyan ath-Thawri, and Fudhail ibn Iyad receive extensive treatment. These were figures whose combination of religious knowledge and spiritual depth made them models for subsequent generations. Al-Hasan al-Basri in particular occupies a central place in the ascetic tradition documented in Sifat as-Safwah: his famous statements about the ephemeral nature of the world, the importance of spiritual accountability, and the qualities required for genuine piety were preserved in dozens of accounts and had a shaping influence on subsequent Islamic spirituality.

The inclusion of women among the notable figures in Sifat as-Safwah is significant. Rabi'ah al-Adawiyyah of Basra, the famous ascetic who articulated a theology of pure love of God, receives extensive treatment. Her accounts — her famous prayer that she loved God not from fear of hell or hope of paradise but purely for His own sake — became central to the Islamic spiritual literature. Ibn al-Jawzi's preservation of these accounts in a biographical format made them accessible across generations.

The spiritual themes that recur through Sifat as-Safwah include: the cultivation of ihsan (spiritual excellence, or worshiping God as if you see Him); the practice of muhasabah (self-accounting, or continuous examination of one's inner states and intentions); the importance of zuhd (asceticism or abstention from worldly attachment); the virtue of sabr (patience under trial); and the centrality of dhikr (remembrance of God) as the animating practice of the spiritual life.
content_ar: The figures who receive the most extensive treatment in Sifat as-Safwah are those who embodied the Sunni Sufi and ascetic traditions at their most distinguished. Reading through these accounts reveals the spiritual themes that Ibn al-Jawzi, following Abu Nu'aym, considered most characteristic of Islamic excellence. The الصحابة receive detailed treatment in the early sections. Their spiritual qualities — the profundity of النبي ﷺ's الصحابة' الإيمان, their sacrifice, their continuous الذكر of God — are documented through accounts of their الصلوات, their supplications, their weeping from الخوف of God, and their treatment of others. The الصحابة are presented not as historical figures only but as living models of how the prophetic message transformed human النفوس. Figures like al-حسن al-Basri, إبراهيم ﷺ ibn Adham, Sufyan ath-Thawri, and Fudhail ibn Iyad receive extensive treatment. These were figures whose combination of religious العلم and spiritual depth made them models for subsequent generations. Al-حسن al-Basri وبالأخص occupies a المحوري place in the ascetic التراث documented in Sifat as-Safwah: his famous statements about the ephemeral nature of the الدنيا, the importance of spiritual accountability, and the qualities required for genuine piety were preserved in dozens of accounts and had a shaping influence on subsequent Islamic spirituality. The inclusion of women among the notable figures in Sifat as-Safwah is المهم. Rabi'ah al-Adawiyyah of Basra, the famous ascetic who articulated a theology of pure المحبة of God, receives extensive treatment. Her accounts — her famous الصلاة that she loved God not from الخوف of النار or الأمل of الجنة but purely for His own sake — became المحوري to the Islamic spiritual literature. Ibn al-Jawzi's preservation of these accounts in a biographical format made them accessible across generations. The spiritual themes that recur through Sifat as-Safwah include: the cultivation of ihsan (spiritual excellence, or worshiping God as إذا you see Him); the practice of muhasabah (self-accounting, or continuous examination of one's inner states and intentions); the importance of الزهد (asceticism or abstention from worldly attachment); the الفضيلة of الصبر (الصبر under الابتلاء); and the centrality of الذكر (الذكر of God) as the animating practice of the spiritual الحياة.
